reasoning: Beto O'Rourke recently (late April/early May 2025) started testing the waters for a 2026 Senate run, stating he will run 'if Texans want him to'. This significantly increases the base likelihood of him running. However, the resolution deadline is July 1, 2025, which is only 40 days from the forecast date. While Senate campaigns often launch in the spring or summer of the off-year (Colin Allred announced for 2024 in May 2023), Beto waited until November 2021 to announce his 2022 gubernatorial run. Furthermore, Colin Allred is reportedly seriously considering another run for the seat and is currently polling favorably, which could cause Beto to either defer to him or wait to consolidate support before declaring, further delaying any announcement. Considering the tight 40-day window, the chance of him deferring/not running due to Allred, and his potential to wait until the fall to announce, the probability of an official announcement before July 1 is estimated at 25%.
